arts.ttl: Roma director Ghisolfi: “We want a club that is not dependent on coaches or directors”
arts.sum: Roma’s new sporting director Florent Ghisolfi has given his first interview since joining the club from Nice, sharing insights on the Friedkins’ vision, upcoming changes, and key player situations. Speaking with La Gazzetta dello Sport (via Calciomercato) Ghisolfi emphasized the owners’ ambitions, stating, “The Friedkins want a great team in the new stadium.” He also addressed rumors about the potential Everton purchase, asserting, “The acquisition of Everton? Nothing will change for Roma.” The club is focusing on building stability and expanding its management structure. Ghisolfi revealed, “We want a club that is not dependent on coaches or directors. arts.ttl: Roma face potential €34 million fine from Italian financial police
arts.sum: Roma are facing a potential hefty fine from the Italian tax authorities following an investigation by the Guardia di Finanza. Calciomercato report that the financial police have scrutinized the club’s financial records from 2016 to 2021, issuing a report alleging “unfaithful declaration” in their accounting practices. The club, owned by Dan and Ryan Friedkin, has so far contested these allegations and refused to settle. arts.ttl: Roma Begin Haggling to Extend the Contract of Pisilli
arts.sum: The breakout performances of Niccolò Pisilli should soon turn into a contract extension since he’s not tied to Roma long-term. arts.ttl: Jimmy Thelin has no Europa League regrets as Aberdeen boss targets big stage after Elfsborg miss
arts.sum: The Swede guided Elfsborg into European competition with his former side playing in Europe's second tier competition
